sentence: Subjective:

The patient is a 62-year-old male who presents with a history of coronary artery disease and a heart attack six months ago, for which he underwent a stent placement in his left anterior descending artery.
He reports being on a fair amount of medications, including aspirin, brilinta, and crestor.
He expresses concern about the number of medications he is taking.
He also mentions that since the heart attack, he experiences dizziness when standing up quickly, which he attributes to either the heart attack or the medication.
He has a history of diabetes, for which he has not been following a diet.
He mentions having a high hemoglobin A1c level of around 16.
He also has stage three chronic kidney disease and has not seen his nephrologist in a year.
He reports having some puffiness in his legs, which he attributes to the medications.
He also mentions being a fan of the San Francisco 49ers and having been lonely since the heart attack.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of coronary artery disease, for which he underwent a stent placement in his left anterior descending artery six months ago.
He also has stage three chronic kidney disease and diabetes.
Review of systems:
The patient reports experiencing dizziness when standing up quickly, which he attributes to either the heart attack or the medication.
He also reports having some puffiness in his legs.
Current medications:
Aspirin [dosage not specified] [frequency not specified] [indication: cardiac health]
Brilinta [dosage not specified] [frequency not specified] [indication: cardiac health]
Crestor 40 mg once a day [indication: cholesterol]
Fish oil [dosage not specified] [frequency not specified] [indication: cardiac health]
Metoprolol 50 mg once a day [indication: cardiac health]
Objective:
Vital signs: blood pressure 127/80 mmHg, pulse rate not specified, oxygen saturation not specified, temperature not specified

Physical exam: jugular venous distention not appreciated, carotid bruits not appreciated, a slight 3/6 systolic ejection murmur heard at the left base, one plus nonpitting edema in the lower extremities
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has coronary artery disease, stage three chronic kidney disease, and diabetes, for which he is being followed by his cardiologist and nephrologist.
Problem list: Coronary artery disease; Stage three chronic kidney disease; Diabetes mellitus
Plan:
I referred the patient for cardiac rehabilitation to help him be more active and confident in exercising again.
I will also refill his metoprolol, crestor, and aspirin.
I did not think it necessary to proceed with a stress test at this time.
I will reach out to Dr. Miller to schedule an appointment for the patient to see his nephrologist.
I will order another hemoglobin A1c and refer the patient to a dietitian for dietary education.
I will also discuss his insulin regimen with his primary care physician.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up with his cardiologist in six months to check on the stent and continue monitoring his cardiac health.
He will also follow up with his nephrologist to monitor his kidney disease.
The patient will also see a psychiatrist to discuss his feelings of sadness and loneliness since the heart attack.
